title: Docker Architecture, OCI Boundaries, Isolation, and Environment Evidence · Deployment Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective container architecture and environment evidence map; isolate the first causal fault in docker architecture, oci boundaries, isolation, and environment evidence and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in container architecture and environment evidence map and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.
Distinguish a container, image, process, namespace, cgroup, virtual machine, and host kernel without overstating isolation.
Separate OCI image, runtime, and distribution contracts from Docker-specific implementation and product behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Client Daemon Api
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine. Competency probe: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.

Inspect the Docker Client Daemon Api trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Client Daemon Api.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace docker cli, contexts, engine api, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Client Daemon Api, keep Docker Client Daemon Api connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: For Docker Client Daemon Api, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Client Daemon Api, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Client Daemon Api precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine. Competency probe: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Client Daemon Api, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: The Docker CLI directly creates isolated processes without a daemon or API.

stimulus.title: Docker Client Daemon Api case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A debug team sees Docker client 29.6.1, an unexpected remote context, Engine API 1.55, containerd-backed image storage, and a process sharing the host kernel. Trace pull and run authority without calling the container a virtual machine. Competency probe: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The Docker CLI directly creates isolated processes without a daemon or API.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace Docker CLI, contexts, Engine API, daemon, containerd, runtime, registry, and host-kernel responsibilities for one operation. Artifact under review: container architecture and environment evidence map.

options.text: For inspect the failure record, copy one familiar Docker command, omit daemon authority, immutable identity, failure, cleanup, and disposable-environment transfer checks, then accept a successful build as production proof. This does not satisfy Docker Client Daemon Api for container architecture and environment evidence map.
The record names the assumption, applies Docker Client Daemon Api, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Docker Client Daemon Api record says container architecture and environment evidence map looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Docker Client Daemon Api.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Version Context Baseline
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-version-context-baseline-1oc2wg". Tailor its architecture-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Version Context Baseline.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Version Context Baseline into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Version Context Baseline, keep Docker Version Context Baseline connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-version-context-baseline-1oc2wg
  - competency: docker-version-context-baseline
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: version context baseline
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: invalidate one cache input without changing application source
    retained-proof: per-platform manifest digest and selected image configuration
    case-key: 1oc2wg
    change: |
      operation: pull
      path: client -> engine-api -> daemon -> containerd -> registry
      kernel-boundary: shared-host-kernel
    verify: docker context inspect training && docker info --format json
    failure: Reject any conclusion based only on the client version or a claim that the container boots a guest kernel.
    repair: Capture client and server versions, active context, platform, runtime, storage driver, and security options separately.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The Docker Version Context Baseline structural check is only one gate. container architecture and environment evidence map must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
